abyssal-ink.$description: Abyssal Ink — Primary text, nav links, borders, icon strokes — the workhorse near-black with a whisper of navy. Carries every reading surface in the system

bone.$description: Bone — Page canvas, card surfaces, button text on dark fills — a warm off-white, never pure #fff

obsidian.$description: Obsidian — Headings, display text, strong UI fills — slightly warmer than Abyssal, used where headings need a touch more warmth

espresso.$description: Espresso — Filled button background (primary action), dark surface sections — the primary CTA color, a warm near-black

carbon.$description: Carbon — Absolute dark for SVG fills and the strictest contrast moments — used sparingly where true black is needed

ash.$description: Ash — Card surfaces, badge backgrounds, subtle elevated panels — the first step up from the canvas

mist.$description: Mist — Borders, dividers, secondary surface fills — a warm gray that defines edges without drawing attention

vapor.$description: Vapor — Lightest visible border, ghost button outlines — the quietest edge in the system

pebble.$description: Pebble — Muted body text, subdued metadata, tertiary information

fog.$description: Fog — Disabled text, placeholder text, very subdued heading accents

smoke.$description: Smoke — Mid-tone surface fills, pressed button states

graphite.$description: Graphite — Tertiary text, subtle borders, icon strokes in secondary contexts

slate.$description: Slate — Body text, nav borders, link underlines — the mid-neutral for readable secondary information

coffee.$description: Coffee — Deep dark surface, image borders, the second-darkest fill — used in footer-adjacent surfaces and image frames

dusk.$description: Dusk — Gradient start — warm golden, the opening tone of the signature sunset gradient

ember.$description: Ember — Gradient mid-stop — coral-orange transition in the signature sunset gradient

twilight.$description: Twilight — Gradient end — muted purple-gray closing the sunset gradient

tt-commons-pro.$description: Functional UI typeface — nav links, button labels, badge text, small labels. Single weight 400 is a deliberate choice: the interface whispers instead of shouts. Used at 16-18px for UI chrome and at 40px for one rare editorial moment.

abc-diatype.$description: Editorial typeface — display headings at 40px weight 500, section headings at 32px weight 500, subheadings at 24px weight 400, body at 16px. The geometric construction gives the system its quiet authority; the weight 500 (not 600-700) for headings is the anti-convention choice that defines Sprig's restraint.

bone-canvas.$description: Surface level 0: Base page background — the warm off-white that defines the entire system's atmosphere

ash-card.$description: Surface level 1: Subtle elevated card surface, badge fills, first step up from the canvas

mist-divider.$description: Surface level 2: Hairline borders, subtle structural dividers between sections

espresso-surface.$description: Surface level 3: Dark filled surfaces — buttons, announcement banners, dark section cards

coffee-depth.$description: Surface level 4: Deepest surface for image frames, footer-adjacent backgrounds, the most recessed visual layer
